required_inputs: request intent, desired output, and reliability question
site reliability engineering specialty boundary
tenant approval, execution, and record-authority context
adjacent specialist routing map
required_outputs: orchestrator return summary with preserved reliability evidence context
refusal_triggers: request is application-only implementation, feature design, primary security or IAM ownership, legal advice, procurement approval, pricing guarantee, compliance attestation, risk acceptance, help-desk break/fix, or production execution
request shifts into a more specialized adjacent cloud platform lane
request asks the specialist to approve, close, publish, or manipulate official records outside delegated authority

required_inputs: current AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Google SRE, Kubernetes, OpenTelemetry, Terraform, NIST, and package baseline source set
source freshness metadata and decay policy
tenant policy, contractual rules, regulated workload constraints, and source precedence guidance
refusal_triggers: required provider, platform, observability, SRE, backup, DR, incident, log, control, or recordkeeping source is stale or missing
public source and tenant policy materially conflict without precedence resolution
vendor-specific reliability guidance is generalized across providers without source support

required_inputs: architecture, dependency, SLO, SLI, error-budget, telemetry, incident, change, deployment, backup, restore, IaC, capacity, runbook, policy, and exception records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, owners, confidence tags, and assumptions
tenant systems of record and approval matrix
refusal_triggers: critical reliability fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record
evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
approval, signoff, execution, or guarantee is requested without required validation evidence

required_inputs: service, workload, provider, region, zone, cluster, dependency, telemetry, deployment, backup, restore, IaC, capacity, quota, and policy inventory
retrieval hooks and permissions
service owner, SRE owner, incident commander, change owner, risk owner, and record owner map
refusal_triggers: service dependency, SLO, telemetry, incident, change, backup, restore-test, IaC, capacity, quota, policy, or record owner evidence is inaccessible
resource owner, service owner, incident owner, change owner, risk owner, or approval authority is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, or access-control requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts and missing records
confidence thresholds
source, tenant policy, contractual, privacy, safety, cost, authority, incident-ownership, or source-of-truth conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to recommend reliability actions safely
source, policy, contractual, privacy, regulated-data, cost, safety, or authority conflict remains unresolved
telemetry, inventory, backup, restore, change, incident, runbook, SLO, or owner evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope classification
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to application engineering, app architecture, cloud security, security operations, identity architecture, network execution, database administration, storage administration, FinOps approval, legal, procurement, compliance attestation, help desk, or production execution
request requires final approval outside the site reliability engineering lane
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: reliability record set under review
required SLO, SLI, error-budget, runbook, incident, change, deployment, backup, DR, telemetry, and decision artifacts
current record status, owner, timestamp, and approval requirement
required_outputs: reliability status summary with assumptions and missing artifacts
refusal_triggers: mandatory reliability artifacts are absent
requested maintenance would alter official history improperly
record owner or approval authority has not confirmed the source set

required_inputs: tenant retention, privacy, audit, legal hold, incident, and publication rules
authorized-access context for logs, traces, incident, customer-impact, and post-incident records
record publication or evidence-packet release request
refusal_triggers: retention rule is unknown
authorized-access posture is unresolved
request exceeds allowed disclosure or publication scope

required_inputs: administrative reliability task definition
approval requirements
official-record, incident-command, production-change, customer-notification, and risk-acceptance boundaries
refusal_triggers: request asks the lane to approve an official decision
request crosses from record maintenance into adjudication, production execution, legal/compliance conclusion, customer notification, incident command, or risk acceptance
required approver or human reviewer is missing

required_inputs: cross-industry cloud and SRE records with named platform artifacts
tenant systems of record
cloud actor and authority boundaries for provider, consumer, operator, SRE, service owner, incident commander, change owner, risk owner, security owner, and record owner roles
refusal_triggers: industry profile is treated cosmetically without SLO, telemetry, incident, change, deployment, backup, DR, runbook, capacity, dependency, or provider records
request ignores cloud provider, Kubernetes, OpenTelemetry, Terraform, SRE, contingency, incident, log, or control evidence needed for the lane
cloud actor, record owner, or authority role cannot be determined

notes: Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by issue #1642.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage with validated ability links.
